userStory: As a guest user, I want the Home page to display guest-state controls and guest-accessible feature buttons, so that I can use the application before login and open the Login page from the Person/Profile button.
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins at: http://localhost/washtabui/home The Home page is the shared landing page for both unauthenticated and authenticated users.
This workflow validates only the guest-state behavior of the existing Home page before login.
The guest state is not a separate page from the authenticated Home state.
The application is launched at http://localhost/washtabui/home - The user is unauthenticated and has not signed in - The Home page loads in guest state
Initial Home page load in guest state
Visibility and interactivity of approved guest-state controls
Visibility and interactivity of shared Home shell controls
Navigation from the Person/Profile button to the Login page or Login view

ownershipConflicts.locator: logged-in user name button not visible

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home page loads successfully in guest state for an unauthenticated user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application loads the shared Home page in guest state, the Home shell is visible, and the session remains unauthenticated without any logged-in user indicators displayed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest-state controls and shared Home shell controls are visible and enabled on Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Person/Profile, Back, Notification, Home, Customer signup, Clean survey, and Site survey buttons are all visible, enabled, and ready for interaction while the Home page remains in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Person/Profile button navigates from Home guest state to Login page or Login view
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Selecting the Person/Profile button transitions the application from Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while preserving unauthenticated status.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Customer signup button navigates to customer-search page from Home guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Clicking the Customer signup button opens the customer-search page and the application remains in an unauthenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Clean survey button navigates to vehicle-survey page from Home guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Clicking the Clean survey button opens the vehicle-survey page and the application remains in guest-accessible unauthenticated mode.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Site survey button navigates to survey page from Home guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Clicking the Site survey button opens the survey page while the user session remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation back to Home guest state after opening Login view from Person/Profile button
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the shared Home page in guest state and all approved guest-state controls remain visible and enabled without authenticating the user.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation back to Home guest state after opening customer-search page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the Home page in guest state with approved guest-accessible buttons still visible and enabled.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation back to Home guest state after opening vehicle-survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page reloads or becomes visible again in guest state and no authenticated user controls become available.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation back to Home guest state after opening survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the Home page guest state and the user remains unauthenticated throughout the workflow.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify repeated clicking of Person/Profile button does not create inconsistent navigation state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application opens only the Login page or Login view without duplicated overlays, broken navigation, or unintended authenticated state changes.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button remains visible and enabled during guest-state execution
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home button is visible and enabled, and clicking it while on the Home page does not break the guest-state session or navigate to an unexpected page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Notification button remains visible and enabled during guest-state execution
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Notification button is visible and enabled and interacting with it does not expose authenticated-only functionality or change the user to an authenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button remains visible and enabled during guest-state execution
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Back button is visible and enabled and interacting with it does not expose authenticated-only content or corrupt the guest-state navigation flow.

validations: The application loads the shared Home page in guest state, the Home shell is visible, and the session remains unauthenticated without any logged-in user indicators displayed.
The Person/Profile, Back, Notification, Home, Customer signup, Clean survey, and Site survey buttons are all visible, enabled, and ready for interaction while the Home page remains in guest state.
Selecting the Person/Profile button transitions the application from Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while preserving unauthenticated status.
Clicking the Customer signup button opens the customer-search page and the application remains in an unauthenticated state.
Clicking the Clean survey button opens the vehicle-survey page and the application remains in guest-accessible unauthenticated mode.
Clicking the Site survey button opens the survey page while the user session remains unauthenticated.
The application returns to the shared Home page in guest state and all approved guest-state controls remain visible and enabled without authenticating the user.
The application returns to the Home page in guest state with approved guest-accessible buttons still visible and enabled.
The Home page reloads or becomes visible again in guest state and no authenticated user controls become available.
The application returns to the Home page guest state and the user remains unauthenticated throughout the workflow.
The application opens only the Login page or Login view without duplicated overlays, broken navigation, or unintended authenticated state changes.
The Home button is visible and enabled, and clicking it while on the Home page does not break the guest-state session or navigate to an unexpected page.
The Notification button is visible and enabled and interacting with it does not expose authenticated-only functionality or change the user to an authenticated state.
The Back button is visible and enabled and interacting with it does not expose authenticated-only content or corrupt the guest-state navigation flow.
transitions: The application loads the shared Home page in guest state, the Home shell is visible, and the session remains unauthenticated without any logged-in user indicators displayed.
The Person/Profile, Back, Notification, Home, Customer signup, Clean survey, and Site survey buttons are all visible, enabled, and ready for interaction while the Home page remains in guest state.
Selecting the Person/Profile button transitions the application from Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while preserving unauthenticated status.
Clicking the Customer signup button opens the customer-search page and the application remains in an unauthenticated state.
Clicking the Clean survey button opens the vehicle-survey page and the application remains in guest-accessible unauthenticated mode.
Clicking the Site survey button opens the survey page while the user session remains unauthenticated.
The application returns to the shared Home page in guest state and all approved guest-state controls remain visible and enabled without authenticating the user.
The application returns to the Home page in guest state with approved guest-accessible buttons still visible and enabled.
The Home page reloads or becomes visible again in guest state and no authenticated user controls become available.
The application returns to the Home page guest state and the user remains unauthenticated throughout the workflow.
The application opens only the Login page or Login view without duplicated overlays, broken navigation, or unintended authenticated state changes.
The Home button is visible and enabled, and clicking it while on the Home page does not break the guest-state session or navigate to an unexpected page.
The Notification button is visible and enabled and interacting with it does not expose authenticated-only functionality or change the user to an authenticated state.
The Back button is visible and enabled and interacting with it does not expose authenticated-only content or corrupt the guest-state navigation flow.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.
